text.title: distribution-channel-research Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for distribution-channel-research as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Trying all channels simultaneously at launch.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Dilutes effort below the minimum needed to get accurate signal from any single channel. Focus on 1-2 initially.

failure-mode.description: Copying competitor channels without validating fit for the specific product.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Channel fit depends on audience presence, cost structure, and team capability — not on what the market leader does.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring channel economics before scaling — no CAC calculation before increasing budget.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Channels that feel productive but have LTV:CAC under 3:1 destroy capital; economics must be modeled before scaling.

failure-mode.description: Giving up on a channel after fewer than 4-6 weeks with no optimization.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Organic channels take 3-12 months to mature; premature abandonment produces false negatives that leave viable channels unexplored.

failure-mode.description: No attribution tracking set up before running channel tests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without attribution, test results cannot be attributed to any channel; the data is useless for decision-making.

failure-mode.description: Channel granularity collapse: "paid social" lumped together as one channel in the market-share table.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Paid social" is not one channel — Meta Reels, Meta Feed, IG Stories, LinkedIn Sponsored, TikTok In-Feed behave differently. Force per-platform breakdown or the analysis is meaningless for channel selection.

failure-mode.description: Survivorship in published case studies: "we got to $10M via SEO" stories overweight organic and underweight paid.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Published stories are 1-of-100 — channel-share inference from public stories must be cross-checked with SimilarWeb traffic mix data from the silent majority.

failure-mode.description: Producing a 7-tier portfolio recommendation ("Phase 1 SEO, Phase 2 paid") in market-research output.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: GTM sequencing is GTM-strategist work, not market research. Stop at channel-share + partner-landscape + economics tables; let the strategist interpret them.

failure-mode.description: Agents skipping the boundary gate and producing "channel mix for SaaS" without geo or segment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a market boundary the channel-share denominator is undefined and all output is conjecture. Enforce a stop-and-ask gate before any data work.

failure-mode.description: Agents fabricating CPC, CPM, take-rate, and traffic-share values without sourced citations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Number hallucination in channel benchmarks produces investment decisions grounded in fiction. Require source + URL + capture-date for every number and reject answers failing the citation check.

failure-mode.description: Boundary skipping: agents will happily produce "channel mix for SaaS" without a geo or segment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Enforce a stop-and-ask gate before any data work; without a market boundary the channel-share denominator is undefined and all output is conjecture.

failure-mode.description: Number hallucination: agents fabricate plausible CPC, CPM, take-rate, and traffic-share values.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require source + URL + capture-date for every number, and reject answers that fail the citation check.

failure-mode.description: US-bias: training data overweights US benchmarks; for non-US markets force the agent to source regional data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For non-US markets force the agent to source regional data (Statista, eMarketer regional, local Ad libraries) rather than defaulting to US benchmarks.

failure-mode.description: Confidence-band omission: agents collapse channel-share into single percentages.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Explicitly require N + confidence band per row; a single percentage projects false precision onto an inherently noisy estimate.

failure-mode.description: Survivorship lean: agents will overweight famous "we won via SEO" stories.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Prompt for the silent majority (incumbents that never wrote a Medium post) by sampling top-10 by traffic, not top-10 by content output.

failure-mode.description: Partner-landscape under-reporting: agents miss MSPs, VARs, and SI partners because those rarely have web presence.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require the agent to scrape "Partners / Resellers / Find a partner" pages of each incumbent and capture every named entity.

failure-mode.description: Equal-weight aggregation bug: agents default to averaging across competitors equally.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Explicitly demand traffic- or revenue-weighted aggregation; equal weights give micro-competitors the same influence as category leaders.

failure-mode.description: LTV point-estimate bug: agents output a single LTV:CAC ratio.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require low/mid/high sensitivity or reject the answer; point estimates hide the cliff where unit economics invert.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require capture-date >= last 12 months for any economic number, else mark [UNVERIFIED].
